mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate keeps discount rates anchored, so equity valuation expands and S&P 500 returns stay positive over the next month when inflation is not accelerating. The signal is confirmed if lower policy rates align with higher 20-day returns and fails if cuts coincide with negative equity reactions.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher when FEDFUNDS is stable or falling and CPIAUCSL inflation is non-accelerating, or if triggered periods show negative average returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply, it signals weakening household income and slower demand, which depresses earnings expectations and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest after a visible labor-market deterioration rather than at already-high unemployment levels.
failure_condition: Rejected if months with UNRATE rising more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months do not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in a majority of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in HOUST and RSAFS indicates improving final demand and retailer sales momentum, which lifts short-horizon equity expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal fails when one series improves but the other remains weak.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint increases in HOUST and RSAFS do not outperform the unconditional 5-day SPX return distribution, or if either series alone explains the effect without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO relative to prior months signals improving industrial output and inventory restocking, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon. The mechanism is falsified when industrial production rises but equities fail to respond positively.
failure_condition: Rejected if episodes with INDPRO up more than 1.0% over 2 months do not show positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is below 50% in triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A low FEDFUNDS rate combined with accelerating CPIAUCSL inflation increases the odds that policy stays accommodative while nominal revenues rise, supporting equity multiples over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when inflation is rising but rates remain pinned near zero.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises
failure_condition: Rejected if low-rate, rising-inflation episodes do not deliver above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the same pattern coincides with persistent negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series signals payroll contraction, which weakens income growth and consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism breaks when payroll declines occur after a strong market rally and are already fully priced.
failure_condition: Rejected if consecutive monthly PAYEMS declines are not followed by below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns dominate triggered cases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth and RSAFS both weaken, aggregate demand is slowing across households and firms, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The claim fails if GDP slowdown alone does not matter unless retail sales also deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1% and RSAFS falls year-over-year
failure_condition: Rejected if combined weak GDPC1 and RSAFS readings do not lead to negative 60-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the effect without a joint interaction.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing contraction regime, a rising UNRATE together with falling INDPRO identifies a sharp cyclical slowdown that compresses earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The pattern should be most predictive when both labor and industrial activity deteriorate at the same time.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint UNRATE up / INDPRO down episodes do not produce below-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect appears equally strong when only one proxy moves.
